title: Rice-Kissed Korean Padron Peppers Bowl
description: A sour and savory gyeongsang dish featuring padron peppers, all cooked in one pot. Served in a scallop shell for a fun twist.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the gochujang and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soy sauce and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add the rice vinegar, sugar, and 2 cups of water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the pot.
Simmer the rice mixture for 18 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is cooked.
While the rice is cooking, heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the padron peppers to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are blistered and charred.
Cut the green onions into thin slices and chop them into smaller pieces.
Once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the chopped green onions.
Serve the rice mixture in a scallop shell, topped with the padron peppers.
